title: High-resolution reconstruction of cell-type-specific transcriptional regulatory processes from bulk sequencing samples - Nature Biotechnology
text:
AbstractSingle-cell sequencing methods such as scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq have advanced our understanding of individual cellular functions but experimentally adapting genome-wide assays measuring other genomic features to achieve single-cell resolution remains a technical challenge. Here we introduce deep-learning-based deconvolution of tissue profiles with accurate interpretation of locus-specific signals (DeepDETAILS), a quasisupervised framework performing cross-modality deconvolution using scATAC-seq reference libraries for other bulk datasets. DeepDETAILS enables base-pair-resolution mapping of genomic signals across diverse cell types, with great versatility for various omics datasets, including nascent transcript sequencing (such as PRO-cap and PRO-seq) and ChIP-seq for chromatin modifications. Using DeepDETAILS, we generated a compendium of high-resolution nascent transcription and histone modification signals across 39 diverse human tissues and 86 distinct cell types. Furthermore, we applied our compendium to fine-map risk variants associated with primary sclerosing cholangitis, a progressive cholestatic liver disorder, and revealed a potential etiology of the disease.

Data availability

All deconvolved tracks are freely available on our portal. Human tissue PRO-cap libraries were deposited to the National Center for Biotechnology Information Gene Expression Omnibus and their accession numbers, together with those of other sequencing datasets retrieved from public databases, are listed in Supplementary Table 1. Cell type annotations for scATAC-seq and snATAC-seq experiments were obtained from the ENCODE portal and the corresponding accession for each deconvolution run is available on our online portal. The data used for the eQTL analysis described in this manuscript were obtained from the GTEx Portal on December 13, 2024. Source data are provided with this paper.
Code availability

The source code of DeepDETAILS is publicly available from GitHub (https://github.com/haiyuan-yu-lab/DeepDETAILS). Scripts used to generate the results reported in this study can also be retrieved from GitHub (https://github.com/haiyuan-yu-lab/DeepDETAILS-analysis).

Additional informationPublisher’s note Springer Nature remains neutral with regard to jurisdictional claims in published maps and institutional affiliations.Extended dataExtended Data Fig. 1 Accessible chromatin is necessary but not sufficient for many regulatory processes.a, Schematic comparison of training strategies used by quasi-supervised methods (for example, DeepDETAILS) and fully supervised models (for example, Puffin-D and Enformer). b, Genome browser view of a locus illustrating that accessible chromatin is required for multiple regulatory processes. c, Genome browser view of a locus illustrating that accessible chromatin alone is insufficient to drive regulatory activity. d–g, Genome-wide joint distribution analyses comparing chromatin accessibility with other regulatory signals, including transcription initiation, pause release, and histone modifications. Chromatin accessibility was measured by ATAC-seq in d and e, and by DNase-seq in f and g. Analyses were performed in K562 cells for d and f, and in GM12878 cells for e and g.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 2 Expanded characterization of DeepDETAILS.a, Architecture of DeepDETAILS (‘seq-only’ version). b, Architecture of DeepDETAILS (‘fused’ version). c–e, Configuration of dilated convolutional blocks (c), heads for predicting the shape of the signal in the ‘fused’ version (d) and total counts in the ‘fused’ version (e). Components with trainable parameters are shown in light colors. Fixed operations are represented in gray. k: number of cell types in the bulk sequencing library, GAP: global average pooling, CAT: concatenation, σ: sigmoid activation, ⊙: element-wise product, ⊕: element-wise summation. f, GPU usage and runtime tracking of DeepDETAILS models when applied to the simulated ‘tissue’ PRO-cap library with three cell types. g, The relationship between the number of parameters in DeepDETAILS and the number of cell types in the bulk sample. h, Workflow of simulating bulk and reference libraries for evaluating the performance of cross-modality deconvolution.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 3 Expanded sequence attribution analysis.DeepDETAILS deconvolved a simulated bulk PRO-cap library consisting of three cell types: K562, GM12878, and MCF-7. The ground-truth signals were labeled as ‘Initiation’, while deconvolved signals from the ‘seq-only’ and ‘fused’ models were shown separately. Sequence motifs contributing to the prediction in each cell type were shown in the ‘I×G’ (input × gradient) tracks. In a, Initiators and TATA boxes (consensus sequences BBCABW/WVTGVV and TATAWAW/WTWTATA, respectively) known to be important for transcription initiation in general were highlighted. In b, motifs associated with cell-type-specific identity were highlighted (for example, IRF-like motif for GM12878, with the consensus sequence GRAAVWGAAAS). B for C/G/T, R for A/G, V for A/C/G, W for A/T, and S for G/C.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 4 Expanded demonstration of DeepDETAILS applied across multiple bulk sequencing assays.a–c, Deconvolution of bulk histone modification signals using DeepDETAILS: a for H3K27ac, b for H3K4me1, and c for H3K4me3. d, Deconvolution of simulated bulk pause-release profiles captured by PRO-seq near gene promoters. Candidate regulatory elements such as enhancers (orange boxes) and promoters (red boxes) were annotated as defined in ENCODE cCRE.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 5 Extended performance comparison of DeepDETAILS with state-of-the-art methods.a, Availability of sequencing libraries for various regulatory steps, with dark color indicating the presence of available libraries (left). Similarity (Pearson’s r) in accessible chromatin landscapes across different cell types as measured by snATAC-seq. b, Evaluation of consistency (Pearson’s r) between Puffin-D reconstructed dominant TSSs and actual dominant TSSs in bulk GRO/PRO-cap libraries. c, Evaluation of consistency (Pearson’s r) between Puffin-D reconstructed dominant pause sites and actual dominant pause sites in 3′ of bulk CoPRO libraries. For b and c, we randomly sampled 5000 peak regions for generating the plots. d and e, Performance comparison between DeepDETAILS and statistical deconvolution tools (1-kb resolution). d for across-cell-type evaluation, and e for the root-mean-square error evaluation (log-transformed counts). In the box plots the center lines, box limits and whiskers denote the median, upper and lower quartiles and 1.5× IQR, respectively.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 6 Extended performance evaluation of DeepDETAILS under challenging cases.a, Performance on bulk and reference libraries with varying sequencing depths. For each technique, two bulk-reference pairs were simulated and deconvolved three times (n = 6 for each bulk sequencing technique). b, Deconvolution of transcription-initiation libraries with increasing numbers of cell types in both bulk and reference libraries. (n = 3 for each simulated setting). c and d, Performance on simulated bulk samples with unbalanced cell-type compositions. X-axis labels indicate the dominant cell-type fraction and the fraction of each of four minor cell types. For each cell composition, six deconvolution runs were conducted per assay. Panel c shows the within-cell-type CCC, and panel d displays the across-cell-type CCC. e and f, Cell-type distributions in real human tissue samples. e, Number of annotated cell types per sample (n = 9790). f, Word cloud of disease terms in the reference, with font size proportional to frequency. g and h, Performance on simulated bulk samples mimicking real human tissue cell-type distributions, with 5 samples per cell-type-number configuration. Panel g shows the within-cell-type CCC, and panel h displays the across-cell-type CCC. i, Performance using unmatched reference libraries, measured by across-cell-type CCC (related to Fig. 4c). For a, b, d, h, and i, CCC was calculated across cell types as in Fig. 3e. In the box plots (e, g, and h) the center lines, box limits and whiskers denote the median, upper and lower quartiles and 1.5× IQR, respectively.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 7 Extended performance evaluation of DeepDETAILS under out-of-distribution conditions.a and b, Examples of DeepDETAILS-deconvolved tracks from a simulated bulk sample containing HCT116 cells in different cellular states: control and auxin-induced CTCF degradation. c, Evaluation of DeepDETAILS’ performances in out-of-distribution scenarios (Supplementary Notes). The left y-axis shows within-cell-type CCC and the right y-axis shows across-cell-type CCC. d and e, DeepDETAILS preserves condition-specific information in an out-of-distribution setting. A normal-condition reference (MCF 10A) was used to deconvolve simulated bulk samples containing either normal (MCF 10A, d) or cancer (MCF-7, e) epithelial cells together with four additional cell types. f, Performance of the preflight check step across different cutoffs. Solid lines represent median values, with shaded areas indicating the 95% confidence interval.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 8 Expanded characterization of DeepDETAILS compendium.a, Dataset composition of DeepDETAILS compendium. b, Variants associated with the trait complement factor B levels are enriched in genomic loci that are specifically active in liver hepatocytes. c, Variants associated with the trait COVID-19 severity are enriched in genomic loci that are specifically active in lung T cells. Two-sided Fisher’s exact test was used to derive the p-values for both b and c. d, Enrichment of signals at marker-gene promoters across 26 cell types. Bars denote cell types, and overlaid points show the corresponding raw values from individual deconvolution experiments; the y-axis indicates the fraction of curated marker-gene promoters with enriched signal relative to other deconvolved cell types from the same bulk sample (n = 560). Values and error bars represent mean and 95% confidence intervals of mean.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 9 Extended analysis for DeepDETAILS bringing new insight into disease etiology.a, Evaluation of the robustness of the inferred macrophage-specific enhancer activity to DeepDETAILS hyperparameter settings and the quality of the reference snATAC-seq input, related to Fig. 6b. b, Gene expression values of PDGFB in different cell types measured by scRNA-seq, with sample sizes of n = 11 for macrophages, n = 11 for endothelial cells, n = 11 for fibroblasts, and n = 1 for hepatocytes. Values and error bars represent mean and 95% confidence intervals of mean. c, Deconvolved promoter signal for PDGFB by different tools. d, Impact of the rs5757584 genetic variant on PDGFB expression in lymphocytes, stratified by genotype (n = 237, 76, and 13 for genotypes CC, CT, and TT, respectively). In the box plot, the center lines, box limits and whiskers denote the median, upper and lower quartiles and 1.5× IQR, respectively.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 10 Extended analysis of DeepDETAILS performance and design principles.a, Cell composition of a human liver sample from snATAC-seq (5012 annotated cells). Cholangiocyte tracks were excluded from Fig. 6 for consistency because preflight checks indicated their absence in some bulk libraries; tracks from libraries containing cholangiocytes are available on the portal. b, Genome browser view of bulk sequencing signals at the PDGFB locus in the right lobe of human liver. c and d, Evaluation of DeepDETAILS’ performance using different hyperparameter values: panel c for ɑ and panel d for batch size. Solid lines represent median values, with shaded areas indicating the 95% confidence interval. e and f, In silico feature-ablation analysis. ‘+’ indicates available features, ‘−’ unavailable features, and ‘~’ shuffled features. n = 4 for each condition. Values and error bars represent median and 95% confidence intervals of median. Panel e for Pearson’s r and panel f for across-cell-type CCC. g and h, Evaluation of DeepDETAILS’ performance when a model is pretrained on one sample and applied to other samples with the same set of cell types but in different proportions. For each assay, we generated four pairs of datasets and conducted the deconvolution process twice for each pair. i and j, Evaluation of DeepDETAILS’ performance in scenarios where rare cell types (ranging from 2.5% to 10%) are present in the bulk sample but absent in the reference (n = 6 for each condition). Panels g and i show the within-cell-type CCC, and panels h and j display the across-cell-type CCC.
